Brisbane Renovation Questions Answered Find answers to the most common questions about home renovations in Brisbane. How much does a full home renovation cost in Brisbane? Full home renovations in Brisbane typically cost $150,000 to $500,000+ depending on scope. A standard 3-4 bedroom home renovation ranges from $250,000-$450,000. Raise and build-under projects start from $650,000. Costs have increased 8-12% in 2025-2026 due to material and labour price rises. Do I need council approval for renovations in Brisbane? Council approval is required for renovations that alter the building footprint or structure. A Building Permit is needed for structural alterations, extensions, and significant layout changes. Internal-only renovations typically do not require planning approval. Properties in Heritage overlay require additional approval. Council approval takes 2-8 weeks depending on complexity. This is general information only. For advice specific to your project, speak with a building certifier or town planner - we can connect you with one if needed. How long does a house renovation take? Full home renovations typically take 5-12 months including approvals. This includes feasibility (2-4 weeks), design and documentation (2-4 months), pre-construction (2-4 weeks), plus construction time. Kitchen renovations take 4-8 weeks, bathroom renovations 2-4 weeks. What is included in a full home renovation? A full home renovation typically includes: structural modifications, kitchen and bathroom upgrades, new flooring, electrical rewirin
